interactive.rationale: Only surface is a feature-detect probe plus illustrative code blocks — no user-driven controls drive the API.

references.rationale: Page cites chromestatus.com/feature/5071996009906176 but the feature's canonical id is 5203950077476864. This breaks the self-heal anchor (CLAUDE.md rule #3) and misleads readers.

design_system.rationale: Inline styles route through --bg/--text/--accent variables, no raw hex, and button overrides pair background + color correctly.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept's lede mirrors the feature index promise — same nouns, same scope.

spec_match.rationale: Concept exercises the keywords the spec/feature-index lede surfaces — covers the headline API surface.

edge_cases.rationale: Page only walks the happy path — there is no visible branch for failure, denial, or invalid input.

openQuestions.title: Add user-driven controls beyond the support probe
openQuestions.detail: The probe is useful but the page reads as 'recipes and a yes/no badge'. Add at least one button that actually fires the API path the recipe describes, and a readout showing what the call returned or what side-effect it had.

openQuestions.title: Fix the chromestatus id link
openQuestions.detail: Replace the citation of chromestatus.com/feature/5071996009906176 with chromestatus.com/feature/5203950077476864. The wrong id will trip the self-heal script and point readers at an unrelated feature.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate at least one failure mode
openQuestions.detail: The page currently has no failure branch. Add a visible second branch that exercises an error path: unsupported browser, permission denied, malformed input, or quota-exceeded. The contrast is what teaches the spec.

summary: Needs work: 2 rubric items fail. Address the major open questions before counting this as a finished concept.
